1.title: Monitor
steps: Return to service.
If system stability issues continue, refer to SA99 - Unidentified Errors and contact AMD for assistance.

2.title: RMA the Failing OAM
steps: For DLC systems, if the event is accompanied with thermal events, warnings, or issues refer to SA11 first.
If the AFID event has not reached the number of failures specified in the threshold key, AC-cycle the system and return the node to service.
Take the node out of service.

Remove the failing OAM (see AMD Instinct [MI3xx] OAM Removal & Installation Guide) and return to AMD, along with the relevant AMD Instinct-generated logs and/or CPER indicating the failure event that maps to an RMA.
Install a new or known good OAM (see AMD Instinct [MI3xx] OAM Removal & Installation Guide).
Optional: Go to SA15 - Stress-test the System to validate the new OAM.

7.title: RMA the Failing UBB Assembly
steps: For DLC systems, if the event is accompanied with thermal events, warnings, or issues refer to SA11 first.
If the AFID event has not reached the number of failures specified in the threshold key, AC-cycle the system and return the node to service.
Take the node out of service.
Remove the failing UBB assembly and return to AMD, along with the relevant AMD Instinctgenerated logs and/or CPER indicating the failure event that maps to an RMA.
Install a new or known-good UBB assembly to the GPU tray.
Go to SA15 - Stress-test the System to validate the new OAM.

10.title: Check and Retry FW Bundle
steps: Verify the checksum of the bundle file to ensure that it is not corrupted. Re-download the FW update binary if needed.
AC-cycle the UBB. Alternatively, if the BMC implementation supports the Redfish or PLDM action to reset the SMC, that method can be used.
If the FW update binary is an older revision than the currently installed version, then this could be an indication that anti-rollback is being enforced for security reasons.
Re-attempt the FW update process by following the AMD Instinct MI300 Series Accelerators Firmware Update Guide.
If the update is successful, put the node back into service.
If the update fails, AC-cycle the UBB and re-attempt the FW update process again by following the AMD Instinct MI300 Series Accelerators Firmware Update Guide.
If the update is successful, put the node back into service.
If the update fails again, then RMA the UBB assembly (see SA7 - RMA the Failing UBB Assembly) or OAM (see SA2 - RMA the Failing OAM).
If the results are inconclusive and the issue persists, contact AMD.

11.title: Check Thermal Solution
steps: Ensure that all platform fans are operational. For DLC, ensure that all liquid pumps, heat exchangers, etc. are operational, and that there are no leaks.

Ensure that a static low fan speed has not been configured on the system. Ensure fan policy is being followed as designed for the chassis based on component. For DLC, ensure that a static low pump speed has not been configured on the system. Ensure pump policy is being followed as designed for the chassis based on component temperature.
Verify data center temperatures are within the acceptable range.
Run AGFHC performance tests using the following command (duration 20 minutes): /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -r thermal
If AGFHC runs successfully, put the node back into service.
If AGFHC fails, see In Band Errors for the appropriate Service Action.
If the system experiences a CTF and the system become unstable, RMA the failing OAM or UBB.
If the system passes thermal testing and continues to have stability issues once returned to service, contact AMD.

13.title: Clear SMC Logs
steps: It is recommended to collect and retain the SMC logs before clearing logs. UBB logs are FIFO and maintain new logs. Full logs don't affect functionality.
Please refer to the Redfish Schema for the latest instructions to clear logs.

15.title: Stress-test the System
steps: AC-cycle the system
Check the ROCm and AGFHC compatibility, and update if incorrect.

To ensure that there are no higher priority events to address first, refer to Redfish Schema on latest instruction on retreiving event entries and CPERs, or refer to publicly available ROCm documentation on amd-smi for collecting CPERs in-band.
Rerun the workload/test that caused the failure.
Run AGFHC using the following command (duration 1 hour): /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -r hbm_lvl4
Run AGFHC using the following command (duration 1 hour): /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -r all_lvl4
If the workload or AGFHC pass and there are no new instances of issues seen, return the system back to service.
If AGFHC or any additional diagnostics fail with additional errors return to step 1 above.
If the workload or AGFHC fails with the same error, RMA the failing OAM (see SA2 - RMA the Failing OAM) or UBB (see SA7 - RMA the Failing UBB Assembly).
If AGFHC test is inconclusive, contact AMD technical support.

16.title: Check PCIe Links
steps: If the AFID event has not reached the number of failures specified in the 'threshold' key, AC-cycle the system and return the node to service.

Validate the PCIe links by running the following AGFHC command. Note if PCIe Link Speed Not Correct, or PCIe Link Width Not Correct, or if there are reported AER: device recovery failed in dmesg. /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -t pcie_link_status

Verify upstream devices (switches, root port) are working properly, by checking for error messages on the host. (Refer to vendor diagnostics.) Resolve any host-related issues and return to step 1. If there are no host-related issues, continue to the next step.
Check the mechanical connection of upstream devices (switches, root port). If issues are found and corrected, return to step 1. If no issues are found, continue to the next step.
Take the node out of service.
Confirm that all four screws on all eight OAMs are torqued as described in the AMD Instinct [MI3xx] OAM Removal & Installation Guide.
Re-install the GPU tray into node.

If the problem still has not been resolved, then replace OAM (OAM location to be detected by OS/ System SEL-based monitoring). Send OS AER logs / System SEL event logs and dmesg with the RMA (refer to SA2 - RMA the Failing OAM).
Refer to SA15 - Stress-test the System.

17.title: Update FW
steps: AC-cycle the system
Check for higher priority AFIDs, CPERs, or other events
Update the BKC by following the AMD Instinct MI300 Series Accelerators Firmware Update Guide.
If a new event occurs while updating refer to SA10 - Check and Retry FW Bundle
If updating the system is not applicable, AC cycle the system and refer to SA1 - Monitor.
If the issue persists after a successful BKC update refer to SA32 - Contact AMD

20.title: Misplaced/Missing OAM
steps: Install the proper SKU OAM into the location; refer to the AMD Instinct [MI3xx] OAM Removal & Installation Guide.

29.title: HPL Isolation
steps: Take the node out of service.
If primary_action_subject is SYSTEM, then run AGFHC using the following command: /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -r rochpl_isolation
If AGFHC fails with another HPL Failure, then RMA the failing OAM (see SA2 - RMA the Failing OAM).

If AGFHC does NOT fail, then RMA the UBB assembly (see SA7 - RMA the Failing UBB Assembly). Note: In this case, the failure arises not from one faulty OAM, but from all eight OAMs running simultaneously.

32.title: Contact AMD
steps: Contact your AMD service provider.

33.title: xGMI WAFL Swap
steps: For MI30XX, and MI32XX products, refer to SA6 - Retorque All OAMs
For MI35XX products, Retrieve the Redfish Event for the failure or collect the AllLogs as described in the Redfish Schema Instructions.
Find the ServiceableUnits from the Redfish Event
If the Redfish Event is not available, refer to Redfish Schema on latest instruction on retrieving event entries and CPERs
In the AllLogs bundle check the redfish-eventlog-entries.json files for AFID 9 or 10 to get the ServiceableUnits.
Retorque the OAMs listed in the ServiceableUnits list.
Return the node to service.
If the failure persists: Swap the affected OAM locations with the unaffected OAM locations, retest to determine the failing OAM. Refer to SA2 - RMA the failing the OAM

98.title: Install JSON
steps: Download the latest version of the AFID_SAG.json package and install.

99.title: Unidentified Errors
steps: AC-cycle the node
Follow SA17 - Update FW.
Follow SA98 - Install JSON with the latest AFID_SAG.json available.
Validate the OAM operation by running the following AGFHC command (duration 1 hour): /opt/amd/agfhc/agfhc -r all_lvl4
If AGFHC runs successfully, put the node back into service.
If AGFHC fails with a different event or AFID, follow the corresponding Service Action.
If the event is still not identified, map the error_category, error_type, and error_severity to an AFID in the AFID_SAG.json to find the appropriate service action.
If the issue persists, refer to SA32 - Contact AMD.
